rulings.Case Title: INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories (Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Special Interrogatories (Set Two). PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED. THE INTERROGATORIES ARE NOT "FULL AND COMPLETE" IN AND OF THEMSELVES. CATANESE V. SUP. CRT (1996) 46 CAL.APP.4TH 1159, 1164. THE INTERROGATORIES ASK DEFENDANT TO AUTHENTICATE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE REFERENCED IN OTHER PLEADINGS. FURTHER, MANY OF THE INTERROGATORIES ARE RELATIVELY UNINTELLIGIBLE IN LIGHT OF THE "REFER TO" DEFINITION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CLEVAND WRECKING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF INTERSTATE FIRE AND CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents (Set One). PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED. THE INCLUSION OF "REFERRED TO" IN THE REQUEST IS CONFUSING. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY MET AND CONFERRED WITH DEFENDANT ON MANY OF THE REQUESTS. FINALLY, DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED MANY OF THE DOCUMENTS. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: PROMETHEUS REAL ESTATE GROUP, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al VS. WOODRUFF-SAWYER & COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Re: Deposition Of Mosa Industrial Corporation, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER REGARDING DEPOSITION OF MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Deft Ands X-Complainant Mosa'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 2, PLAINTIFF UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS, DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND MARKETING, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT MOSA'S DEPOSITION IN THE COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT MOSA. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. CCP SECTION 2025.250 CONTROLS. THE DEPOSITION(S) WILL TAKE PLACE IN SAN FRANCISCO AND WILL BE ALLOWED TO TAKE PLACE AFTER THE DISCOVERY CUT OFF DATE OF AUGUST 12, 2011 BUT MUST TAKE PLACE BY AUGUST 30, 2011. ALL TOPICS DISCUSSED IN THE PAPERS ARE RELEVANT AND MUST BE COVERED IN THE DEPOSITION(S). REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TED MURRI et al VS. ALEJANDRO CALUGAY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Opposing Deft Justin Goree'S Application For Good Faith Determination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 4, DEFENDANT ALEJANDRO CALUGAY'S Motion Opposing Defendant Justin Goree's Application For Good Faith Determination. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTEST GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED. THERE IS INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE IN THE RECORD FOR THE COURT TO CONCLUDE THAT THE SETTLEMENT IS IN THE BALLPARK. MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARTHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1350, n.6. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TED MURRI et al VS. ALEJANDRO CALUGAY et al

ARTHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1350, n.6. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CLEANWELL COMPANY VS. JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Prevailing Party Attorneys' Fees

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEY MUST FILE A DETAILED TIME RECORD ACCOUNTING FOR ITS FEES. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Inspection And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 27, 2011, line 3, DEFENDANTS IVAN BURGOS M.D., HERIBERTO OCASIO M.D. Amended Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Inspection And Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions. THE COURT VACATES THE ORDER OF JUNE 16, 2011 GRANTING THE MOTION TO COMPEL, AS IT WAS ENTERED IN ERROR. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO INTERROGATORIES 16 AND 17. IT IS GRANTED AS TO ALL OTHER INTERROGATORIES AND RFP'S. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. =(302/LMG)
